$schema_comment: TinyBolus formulary. Edit values here to correct doses/ranges; the app picks up changes on next launch (via remote fetch). Item types: 'linear' (factor * weight), 'linear_range' (low..high * weight), 'linear_max' (factor * weight, capped at max), 'fixed' (string value, no calc), 'computed' (dispatches to a named calculator in lib/calculators/). All weights in kg. Units: mg, µg (microgram), mL, mL/h, J, cm.
$references_comment: Normalized citation map. Items reference entries here by key (see item.references: [...]). The About screen resolves each key to the full citation text. Keys are short but readable; pattern: first-author-or-body_topic_year.

items.name: Compression depth
items.note: not exceeding 6 cm at any age

items.name: C:V ratio (2 rescuers)
items.note: 1 rescuer: 30:2

items.name: Defibrillation
items.note: escalate to 8 J/kg (max 360 J) for refractory VF/pVT from the 6th shock

items.name: Cardioversion (synchronised)
items.note: double per attempt to max 4 J/kg

items.name: Adrenaline (cardiac arrest)
items.note: repeat every 3–5 min

items.name: Amiodarone
items.note: max 150 mg for subsequent doses

items.name: Atropine
items.note: 20 µg/kg · max single dose 0.5 mg child / 1 mg adolescent · cumulative max 2 mg · no minimum (Barrington 2011)

items.name: Adenosine (2nd dose)
items.note: if SVT persists, escalate in 0.05–0.1 mg/kg steps to a max single dose of 0.5 mg/kg (ERC 2025)

items.name: Tidal Volume
items.note: intraoperative ventilation, healthy lungs · use lower end (5 mL/kg) for neonates and very young infants

items.name: Propofol
items.note: after a sevoflurane inhalational induction, roughly halve the dose (lower requirement)

items.name: Ketamine (IM)
items.note: induction/sedation when no IV access · onset 2–5 min

items.name: Rocuronium
items.note: intubation 0.6 · RSI 1.0–1.2

items.name: Succinylcholine (RSI)

brackets.note: raised in infants — larger extracellular-fluid volume of distribution (Meakin 1989)

items.note: intubating dose · laryngospasm + IM doses in Respiratory

brackets.note: neonate: reduce dose · inject slowly (chest-wall rigidity) · monitor for apnoea (SPA 2019)
young infant <6 mo: reduced clearance — prolonged effect · slow injection · hypotension, esp. with an opioid

items.name: Midazolam (IN premed)
items.note: intranasal premedication · 0.3 mg/kg for faster, more reliable onset

items.name: Dexamethasone (anti-inflammatory)
items.note: higher anti-inflammatory / airway dose (vs the 0.15 mg/kg PONV dose above)

items.name: Etomidate
items.note: haemodynamically stable induction

items.name: Atracurium
items.note: intubating dose · 0.3–0.4 mg/kg in infants

items.name: Dexmedetomidine (IN premed)
items.note: intranasal premedication · onset 30–45 min

items.name: Paracetamol
items.note: IV dosing splits by weight, not age (SmPC): ≤10 kg → 7.5 mg/kg

brackets.note: neonate: reduce dose · inject slowly · monitor for apnoea (SPA 2019)
reduced in infants <1 y — higher exposure per dose from immature metabolism (Ziesenitz 2019)

items.name: Ibuprofen (oral)
items.note: analgesic / antipyretic · typically 7–10 mg/kg/dose

items.name: Adrenaline (cardiac arrest)
items.note: repeat every 3–5 min

items.name: Adrenaline (anaphylaxis IM)
items.note: weight-based 10 µg/kg (shown) · autoinjector equivalents: <6 y 150 µg · 6–12 y 300 µg · >12 y 500 µg (EAACI)

items.name: Adrenaline (anaphylaxis IV)
items.note: titrate to response · IM adrenaline is first-line

items.name: Atropine
items.note: 20 µg/kg · max single dose 0.5 mg child / 1 mg adolescent · cumulative max 2 mg · no minimum (Barrington 2011)

brackets.note: young infants need ~10× the adult dose (indirect agent · immature SNS) · 1.2 mg/kg optimal (Szostek 2023)

items.name: Phenylephrine
items.note: vasopressor — hypotension (also tet spells)

items.name: Lidocaine
items.note: antiarrhythmic — alternative to amiodarone for shock-refractory VF/pVT

items.name: Glucose 10%
items.note: 0.2 g/kg for hypoglycaemia · recheck glucose after

items.name: Calcium gluconate 10%
items.note: hyperkalaemia · hypocalcaemia · CCB toxicity · give slowly

items.name: Sodium bicarbonate 8.4%
items.note: 1 mL/kg of 8.4% · for hyperkalaemia or TCA toxicity, not routine arrest

items.name: Dantrolene
items.note: repeat 5–10 min · max 10 mg/kg

items.name: Maintenance
items.note: 4-2-1 rule (Holliday-Segar)

items.name: ETT
items.note: for uncuffed tube, add 0.5 to size

items.name: ETT insertion depth
items.note: nasal: add 2–3 cm

items.name: Laryngoscope blade
items.note: infants: straight (Miller) blade preferred to lift the floppy epiglottis directly · Macintosh 0–1 an accepted alternative

items.name: Face mask
items.note: mask should cover nose and mouth from eyebrow line to chin cleft · anatomical fit is more important than estimated size

items.name: Suction catheter
items.note: roughly twice the ETT internal diameter (Fr)

items.name: Salbutamol (inhaled)
items.note: 100 µg/puff · repeat incrementally as needed

items.name: Salbutamol (nebulized)
items.note: repeat back-to-back or continuously per response

items.name: Ipratropium (inhaled)
items.note: 20 µg/puff · repeat as needed

items.name: Succinylcholine (IM)

brackets.note: raised in infants — larger extracellular-fluid volume of distribution (Meakin 1989)

items.note: for laryngospasm or RSI without IV access

items.name: Rocuronium (laryngospasm)
items.note: when succinylcholine is unavailable or contraindicated

items.name: Naloxone (titrated)
items.note: post-anaesthesia reversal — titrate to respiration · repeat every 2–3 min

items.name: Naloxone (overdose)
items.note: suspected opioid overdose with apnoea or unresponsiveness

items.name: Flumazenil
items.note: max bolus 200 µg · total max 1 mg

items.name: Sugammadex
items.note: 2 mg/kg moderate block · 4 mg/kg deep block

items.name: Sugammadex (profound block)
items.note: for CICO / immediate reversal of full intubating-dose rocuronium

items.name: Neostigmine
items.note: coadminister with atropine to block muscarinic effects · give slowly over 1–2 min after first signs of spontaneous recovery

items.name: Atropine (with neostigmine)
items.note: give together with or just before neostigmine to blunt bradycardia and bronchorrhoea

items.name: Intralipid 20% bolus
items.note: max total 12 mL/kg

weight_estimation.comment: APLS-updated. <1 month requires user input. >12 years requires user input.

ett_rules.comment: Neonatal weight-based (table below, widened to ≤4 kg in v0.0.37), then infant age table (v0.0.43: split 1–6 mo → 3.5 / 7–12 mo → 4.0 cuffed, per Duracher/Motoyama age/4 + 3.5 — the old flat 4.0 oversized young infants), then CUFFED formula 3.5 + age/4 (uncuffed adds 0.5 — see item note). Round formula result both up and down to nearest 0.5. infant_under_1y_size is kept as a legacy fallback so pre-v0.0.43 app binaries can still parse this file.

lma_table_comment: Bands overlap intentionally per v1 formularium. A patient in an overlap region shows both sizes; a patient in a single band additionally shows the next size up. min/max are INCLUSIVE on both ends.
auragain_table_comment: Ambu AuraGain sizing per IFU V7 (August 2023). Bands only overlap at boundary weights (5, 10, 20, 30, 50, 70, 100 kg) because min/max are INCLUSIVE on both ends, matching the lma_table convention so computeLma renders both tables consistently.

compression_depth.comment: Age-piecewise. Applies to the PALS section.
